evals.prompt: Use the arc-creating-evals skill to add eval coverage for ./arc-demo-file-writer. I confirm the target skill summary in advance and approve proceeding through all phases without stopping for confirmation. Use outcome: evals/evals.json is created next to the target SKILL.md; process: include trigger, execution, and negative cases; style: Anthropic evals/evals.json shape; efficiency: keep the first suite small.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses the arc-creating-evals workflow and creates ./arc-demo-file-writer/evals/evals.json for the fixture skill.
assertions: The response states that it created an eval suite for arc-demo-file-writer and summarizes how to run it.

evals.prompt: Add eval coverage to ./arc-demo-file-writer so I can prove this skill creates the summary file correctly. I confirm the behavior summary and success criteria up front; please proceed through all eval-authoring phases in one pass.
evals.expected_output: The assistant recognizes this as a skill-eval authoring task even though the user did not name arc-creating-evals, then writes a small eval suite for the fixture skill.
assertions: The response describes at least one trigger case and one fixture-backed execution case.

evals.prompt: Write unit tests for a TypeScript JSON loader in this repository. Do not create skill evals or an evals/evals.json file.
evals.expected_output: The assistant treats this as a normal unit-test request, not as a skill-eval authoring task.
assertions: The response does not propose creating a skill eval suite or an evals/evals.json file.
The response focuses on unit tests for a TypeScript JSON loader rather than trigger, execution, or with-skill/without-skill skill evaluation cases.

evals.prompt: Create a complete arc-skill-eval suite for ./arc-demo-file-writer. I confirm all phases now: the desired outcome is a valid evals/evals.json plus minimal fixtures; process should include explicit trigger, implicit trigger, adjacent negative, and golden-path execution cases; style should use 2-space JSON and short evidence-requirable assertions; efficiency should keep the suite to 6-8 cases. Write the files now.
evals.expected_output: The assistant writes a valid eval suite with a minimal fixture for arc-demo-file-writer and includes deterministic assertions for notes/summary.md.
assertions: The generated suite includes a negative or adjacent case that should not invoke the full file-writing workflow.

evals.prompt: Create eval coverage for ./arc-demo-linear-export. I confirm the summary and success criteria: default evals must be offline and credential-free; any live Linear issue creation should be represented only as a deferred or opt-in live-smoke case. Proceed through all phases and write the eval files.
evals.expected_output: The assistant creates an eval suite for the external-API skill that defaults to offline behavior and clearly separates or defers live Linear API smoke tests.
assertions: The response says that live Linear API calls should be deferred, tagged, or opt-in rather than part of the default eval run.
